name: Boldr — Workflow 3: Weekly Theme Clustering

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({ model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6', max_tokens: 2000, messages: [{ role: 'user', content: 'You are a customer intelligence analyst for Boldr Supply Co., a Singapore titanium watch micro-brand.

Below are the customer questions from the past 7 days. Group them by theme.

For each theme return:
- Theme name
- Ticket count
- Buyer personas represented
- Whether it is a marketing signal (true/false)
- One-line recommended action

Also flag themes that appeared 3+ times as strong signals.

RETURN JSON ONLY — no markdown, no preamble:
{
  "week_summary": "one sentence",
  "themes": [{"theme": "string", "count": 0, "personas": [], "marketing_signal": false, "recommended_action": "string"}],
  "top_signals": ["string"],
  "new_gaps_count": 0,
  "answered_rate_pct": 0
}

TICKET DATA:
' + JSON.stringify($json.data, null, 2) }] }) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({ cluster_id: $json.cluster_id, week_ending: $json.week_ending, themes_json: $json.themes_json, top_signals: $json.top_signals, new_gaps_count: $json.new_gaps_count, resolved_gaps_count: $json.resolved_gaps_count }) }}

parameters.text:
={{ '📊 *WEEKLY CS INTELLIGENCE DIGEST* — Week ending ' + $json.week_ending + '

' + $json.week_summary + '

*Stats:* ' + $json.total_tickets + ' tickets | ' + $json.new_gaps_count + ' new gaps | ' + $json.answered_rate_pct + '% answered by AI

*Top signals this week:*
' + ($json.top_signals || []).map(s => '• ' + s).join('
') + '

*Themes:*
' + ($json.themes_json || []).map(t => '• *' + t.theme + '* (' + t.count + ' tickets) — ' + t.recommended_action).join('
') }}

notes:
Schedule: Monday 00:00 UTC = 08:00 SGT
OPTION B: All credentials hardcoded.
CODE NODE FIX: n8n task runner Code nodes cannot use $('NodeName') — only $input.
FIX: Three Set/Code nodes bridge data:
  • Collect Gaps (Code, runOnceForAllItems): aggregates N gap items → 1 item with gaps_json string. Prevents Fetch Tickets from running N times.
  • Set Tickets With Gaps (Set, includeOtherFields:true): attaches gaps_json to each ticket item from $('Collect Gaps').first().json
  • Set Cluster Meta (Set, includeOtherFields:true): re-attaches cluster_id/week_ending/totals after Claude API replaces input
Code nodes now only use $input.first().json or $input.all() — no $() cross-references.
